title: Unacademy Centre - The Power Behind You
description:
There was the power of a billion people cheering Sachin🔥 
Let Unacademy Centre be the power behind you 💙

Unacademy Centres are now in 40+ cities across India, learn more here:
Unacademy Centres in 40+ cities for JEE and NEET https://unacademy.onelink.me/M2BR/r2q8ciiu

No one will cheer for you louder than we do.
No one will rally behind you better than we do. 

At the Unacademy Centre get the power of,

⭐️A community of passionate peers who cheer you on. 
⭐️India's Best Educators who inspire you to learn better. 
⭐️The right learning environment that fuels your desire to excel. 
⭐️Mentors who believe in you more than you believe in yourself. 

To every dreamer, believer, and achiever out there, we are behind you. 

#UnacademyCentre #unacademy #PowerBehindYou
transcript: [Applause] [Applause] [Music] Le me there was the power of a billion people cheering for me what's the power behind you

title: Epic Hide & Seek Ft. @ashishchanchlanivines @SamayRainaOfficial  | Official Challenge
description:
A Hide & Seek battle you have never experienced before.
Watch the two most popular grown-up kids on the internet, Ashish Chanchlani and Samay Raina battle it out with their teams in the ‘Epic Hide & Seek’ challenge.

40 children.
2 seekers.
1 massive college campus.
Let the game begin.

Comment your favourite epic moment from the video.

#UnacademyEpicHideAndSeek #Unacademy #HideAndSeek #AshishChanchlani #SamayRaina

title: Epic Hide & Seek Ft. @ashishchanchlanivines  @SamayRainaOfficial | Official Trailer
description:
Watch the most insane Hide & Seek battle as @ashishchanchlanivines and @SamayRainaOfficial take on each other.

There is a whole lot of frenzy, exciting twists, ‘tu mujhe nahi hara sakta’ energy and 40 children hiding in one of the biggest college campuses in India. Do we need to say anything more?

Keep taking that wild guess of who you think would be the champion.
Team Ashish or Team Samay?

Video launching soon.

#UnacademyEpicHideAndSeek #Unacademy #HideAndSeek #AshishChanchlani #SamayRaina

title: Unacademy Connect: Defence as an aspiring Career| Lt Gen SL Narasimhan, PVSM, AVSM*, VSM, PhD (Retd)
description:
Unacademy Connect is a series of ‘Career Guidance’ sessions where students come together from across all platforms, backgrounds and geographies to understand emerging career opportunities and to hear from experienced industry practitioners, mentors and seasoned professionals from a variety of sectors. The aim of this series is to help young learners to find their career calling and take well-informed career decisions.
This is an extremely insightful session on how Defence can be an aspiring career where Lt Gen SL Narasimhan, PVSM, AVSM*, VSM, PhD (Retd), shares his experiences and stories which will motivate and inspire many to join one of India’s most prestigious professions.
transcript: [Music] a very good morning to everyone a warm welcome to our esteemed speaker and the audience who have joined us today i welcome everyone to unacademy connect today where we are going to discuss about how defense is an aspiring career and i'm delighted and honored to welcome a very very special guest lieutenant general sl narasimhansar thank you so much sir for joining today there are hundreds of students who have joined from across the country from tripura to himachal to telangana to karnataka and from various classrooms and are very excited to hear from our very special guest unfortunately there are some who couldn't join due to some bad bad weather and they have requested for a recording session as well lieutenant general essen narasimhansa pvsm avsm bsm is an infantry officer commissioned in 1977 he has done graduation in mathematics post graduation in defense studies and phd in india china relations and he has been awarded four times for his outstanding contribution to the indian army by the president of india general narasimhan has been an instructor in the indian military training team in bhutan he has been an avid china watcher for the last 20 years narasimhansar served as the defense attache in the embassy of india in china for three years his expertise spans international relations and internal issues economy and defense related subjects of china lieutenant general narasimhansar has taken part in many track 2 dialogues both in india and abroad and he has authored many articles in various journals and magazines and he is presently member of the national security advisory board india and director general center for contemporary china studies he is also a distinguished fellow with the center for air power studies india thank you so much once again sir for your time today we are privileged to have you we also have shiv shukla director of public policy and government affairs at an academy in his past assignments he was in ministry of commerce government of india meet the ioc and various other private organizations as well and he comes with both experience both government and as well as various fields in industry thank you so much sriv over to you uh thank you so much namaste so i have known uh sir so i will follow the protocol today i will not call you uncle so i have known him for many many years now and he has been an inspiring figure i look up to him for you know so many uh suggestions advices and also mr simon she has been a great inspiration for me in many ways but today i'll follow the protocol of defense services and i'll call him sir so uh just like how ankita was sharing uh students from 9th 10th till graduation they are very very excited to listen to you uh how we have sort of kept the entire session in three segments so one will be we would just request you to share your own journey of different services your own preparation days uh normally we call it oil cues offices like qualities and we have to look to defend services so a bit about that and then i'll have few questions which i have received from students from educators and i have a couple of questions of my own as well so perhaps for next seven to eight minutes time uh it's the floor is open and you can please just share your experience thank you over to you okay thank you very much to an academy and shiv shukla for getting me over to this i always look for opportunities in to interact with young minds which is actually the future of our country and that is something which i never refuse to talk to them whenever the opportunities come up uh when when mr shiva asked me a couple of days ago about this particular interaction i thought i'll definitely let me start by saying what motivated me to join the armed forces when i was studying in 11th standard because those days 10 plus 2 plus 3 was not the system 11 plus 1 plus three was the system so i was studying in the school final that is in 1972 that is the time the 71 war 7172 that is the one that is the time the 71 war happened and that motivated me to think about joining the armed forces my favorite subject was mathematics so i thought i i will pursue graduation till mathematics and thereafter then see if i can join the armed forces when i thereafter i joined the ncc in in the college in the second year pre-university it was not allowed on the first year when i joined the degree course at the bse mathematics that time i joined the ncc that gave me further motivation to join the armed forces at a later stage and when i came to the final year i took part in the republic day paradise and ncc carried from the terminal and pondicherry contingent and when i was marching down the rajput in 1976 as a young cadet who was um who was getting motivated slowly and steadily to join the armed forces after that when i went back it is the motivation that i got from my college that actually helped me how it helped me i'll tell you uh you know when i came for the republic day parade that was the last year of my degree that is the third year of my degree and my principal was very uncomfortable sending me for the republic day parade basically he thought he will i'll not get the first class as they used to call it at that time in the in the degree course he actually made me and my father sit in front of him and took a commitment from me that i'll come back study hard and get a first class again so that he did when i came back unfortunately what happened to me was the vector analysis sec chapter section of the course did not start when i left when it came back and early finished so the the the lecturers in the college took it on themselves to give me extra classes to ensure that i covered that subject also when i went in for the final exams and i did get a first class as i promised to the principal then when i went for the services selection board interview as a ncc cadet i i applied through upsc i also applied through ncc uh through the ncc at that time the ncc certificate used to be an entry at that time so he asked me he called me and asked me that you know whether i needed some help so i told him sir my arms are weak i need to strengthen them because in the services selection board interview you have a monkey rope which you need to cross so for that i need help lo and behold by evening the physical director of the college was standing in front of a huge tree that was in the college and putting a vertical rope for me to climb and practice so this is the kind of support i got from my mentors at that time and that motivated me further not to fail them so when i finally cleared the services selection board and i took the ncc route to join the academy that was the turning point in my life and that thereafter i never looked back actually speaking so there were many courses that you need to do when you and then in the academy it was a one and a half year course because i was a direct entry candidate and so i had to do one and a half degrees whereas others who came from the national defense academy had to do only one year because they had already done three years of training in the national defense academy so that was another motivation to compete with them and do well and thereafter of course there was no there was another thing which came up in the training as to which armor service that you need to select so i had no doubts in my mind that i will join the infantry which is the frontline frontline kind of a force that always gets into when anything that happens to our country so infantry was the choice amongst the infantry then the problem came up as to which regiment should i opt for so then of course you get motivated by the number of instructors in the training academy you see the demeanor you see the way they behave and so you get impressed by those regiments so then initially i opted for a few regiments three of them i opted for gorkas kumao and probably gadwal regiment and then in the final year when they came up with another change of choice if you want to change your choice you can change it and as a matter of fact my drill used to be good so my drill instructors were very impressed by it and so i put gods as my first regiment to be selected second i put the madras regiment and third of course i went into the gorkhas again but ultimately as luck would have it i got commissioned into the madras regiment and thereafter of course i have no regrets of joining the regiment and i have done i think reasonably well for myself in the in the services the the the in the service i don't want to take much time i want to give more time for question answers i'll just finish with this the service took me to many parts of the country we have a two year on and off peace and field it took me to many parts of the country and i almost other than orissa i think i have served in almost all the states in the country and it also took me to some people generally don't go but i had the opportunity of going there too a number of times so with all this i retired as a lieutenant general which is the number two rank in the in the armed forces precisely because once you become a lifting lifting general it's a date of birth that decides your further promotion so that i wish i was born a little later i could have probably reached the top but
